w:r.w:t: Claude Code Project Brief · May 2026 · Confidential

StoryClue is an AI-powered crossword puzzle generator available at StoryClue.ai. It creates grade-appropriate, validated crossword puzzles on demand from any chapter, book, or topic. A homeschool parent pastes a chapter, selects a grade level, and gets a playable puzzle in seconds. A teacher generates a printable worksheet on the fly. A senior solves a puzzle built from the book she is currently reading.
No existing tool generates grade-adapted crosswords from raw educational content automatically. StoryClue fills that gap.
These standards must be applied consistently throughout the entire application. Claude Code must not deviate from these without explicit instruction.
StoryClue (one word, capital S and C)
https://storyclue.ai — use in all meta tags and SEO
3.3 million homeschool students in the US. Parents spend $700-$1,800 per child per year on curriculum tools. Religious homeschool families are a critical high-value segment:
Christian homeschool families — largest segment, curriculum tied to Biblical literacy
Catholic homeschool families — classical and religious education tradition
Jewish homeschool families — Torah study, Hebrew vocabulary, Jewish history
These families share tools virally through co-ops, curriculum networks, and Facebook groups
7 million teachers on Teachers Pay Teachers actively buying classroom materials. Price sensitive. Target $4.99/month.

Recall-based crosswords built from a senior's current book. Discovered when Carole (84-year-old research librarian) used the demo as a cognitive recall exercise. Target: memory care, assisted living, senior book clubs, occupational therapists.
User pastes chapter text, vocabulary list, or topic description
Anthropic Claude Sonnet API extracts vocabulary and writes grade-appropriate clues
crossword-layout-generator npm library places words on the grid
Full validation runs before any puzzle renders — see Section 7
Minimum 15 words, maximum 25 words per puzzle for best grid density
Same answer words. Same grid. Only the clue language changes. Core differentiator — no existing tool does this.
User selects which books in a series they have read from a pre-built checklist. AI generates puzzle using only vocabulary from selected books. Never references events from unread books.
The Reacher Series by Lee Child (Books 1-27)
Charlotte's Web and E.B. White works
The Bible — by book and testament
Full screen — app fills entire device screen
Top panel 60% — crossword grid, scrolls independently in both directions
Colored divider bar — forest green gradient
Bottom panel 35% — clue list with Across/Down tabs, scrolls independently
Active clue shown at top of clue panel when cell is selected
Mobile: 30px cells on phone, 42px on iPad and desktop
Mobile keyboard: grid scrolls to keep active cell visible above keyboard
Timer starts on first keystroke — shown in header
Mistake counter — increments on wrong letters
Progress bar — percentage of grid filled correctly
Check Answers — green correct, red wrong, yellow empty
Win condition: all cells correct. Revealed puzzles cannot trigger win.
Win banner shows final time and mistake count
CSS @media print stylesheet — clean black and white
Name, Grade, Date fields at top of printed page
No UI chrome in printed version
Share button copies StoryClue.ai puzzle URL to clipboard
Puzzle encoded in URL parameters — no database required for v1
Recipient opens URL and sees identical puzzle ready to solve
Bible chapter vocabulary — King James Version (public domain)
Bible story puzzles — David and Goliath, Creation, Noah, Exodus etc.
Torah portion vocabulary for Jewish families
Catholic saints and history for Catholic families
User selects faith tradition — no mixing of traditions in one puzzle
Win messages are faith-appropriate — Well Done Good and Faithful Solver
King James Version is public domain — modern translations are not
Extract vocabulary only — never reproduce full scripture passages
SEO must be built into StoryClue.ai from day one. Claude Code must implement every item in this section.
6.3 HTML Meta Tags — Required in index.html
Title: StoryClue — AI Generated Crossword Puzzle Maker for Kids, Teachers and Seniors
Meta description: StoryClue.ai creates grade-appropriate crossword puzzles instantly from any chapter or book. AI-powered, spoiler-safe, K-6 grade levels. Free for homeschool families and teachers.
Meta keywords: crossword puzzle maker, AI crossword generator, homeschool crossword, educational crossword puzzle, crossword maker for kids, Bible crossword puzzle
Open Graph title: StoryClue — AI Crossword Puzzle Maker
Open Graph description: Paste any chapter. Get a crossword puzzle in seconds. Grade-adaptive clues for K-6. Perfect for homeschool, classroom, and seniors.
Open Graph image: branded StoryClue.ai puzzle screenshot
description: AI Generated Crossword Puzzle Maker for kids, teachers, and seniors
sitemap.xml — list all pages at storyclue.ai
robots.txt — allow all crawlers, point to sitemap
404 page — branded StoryClue.ai, links back to home
HTTPS — Vercel provides automatically for storyclue.ai
Core Web Vitals — test with Google PageSpeed Insights after deployment
Hero: StoryClue — The AI Crossword Puzzle Maker That Reads Your Chapter
Subheading: Paste any chapter. Select a grade level. Get a crossword puzzle in seconds.
Section: How It Works — 3 steps with keywords in headers
Section: Faith-Based Learning — Bible crossword puzzle maker
FAQ section — each question is a long-tail keyword
Testimonial section — add after beta launch
How do I make a crossword puzzle from a book chapter?
What is the best AI crossword puzzle maker for teachers?
Can I make a crossword puzzle for free?
How do I create a homeschool crossword puzzle?
What crossword puzzle maker works for elementary school?
How do I make a Bible crossword puzzle?
Can I print a crossword puzzle I made online?
How do I make a crossword puzzle for seniors?
What is the best crossword puzzle maker for kids?
App name: StoryClue — Crossword Puzzle Maker
Subtitle iOS: AI Generated Puzzles for Any Book
Short description Android: AI crossword puzzle maker for any book, chapter, or topic. Grade K-6 clues. Perfect for homeschool and classroom.
Every grid must pass all validation checks before rendering. Non-negotiable.
Use crossword-layout-generator npm library — never manual word placement
Confirm every word intersects at least one other word — no islands
Confirm every intersection has matching letters — no conflicts
If validation fails: auto-retry once with same word list
If retry fails: show friendly error — never render broken puzzle
Validation runs server-side before puzzle sent to client
Show: Could not generate puzzle. Please try again.
Show: Try pasting more chapter content.
Auto-retry once. If still failing show friendly error.
Grid scrolls up to keep active cell visible above keyboard.
No win banner. Revealed puzzles cannot trigger win.
None in v1. All puzzles anonymous. No login required.
Identical layout. Test on both before launch.
Vercel serverless function — key never in client code
Vercel — free tier, auto-deploy from GitHub
None in v1 — URL encoding for share feature
Google Fonts — Playfair Display and Lora
The Anthropic API key must never appear in client-side code. All API calls route through a Vercel serverless function at storyclue.ai/api/generate.
Body: { chapterText: string, grade: string, seriesBooks?: string[] }
Response: { title: string, words: [{ word: string, clue: string, direction: string }] }
Words must be ALL CAPS, letters only, no spaces, no punctuation
Clues written at specified grade level
Return only valid JSON — no markdown, no preamble
Religious content clues must be respectful and faith-appropriate
Domain StoryClue.ai is registered and confirmed. Point DNS to Vercel after account setup.
Create free GitHub account at github.com
Create free Vercel account at vercel.com
Connect StoryClue.ai domain DNS to Vercel — instructions at vercel.com/docs/projects/domains
Create new GitHub repository — push all project files
Connect GitHub repo to Vercel — auto-deploy on every push
Check if Bob already has Anthropic API key from Reonomy project
If not, get key at console.anthropic.com
Add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY as environment variable in Vercel project settings
Implement all SEO meta tags, structured data, sitemap.xml, robots.txt
Verify canonical URL https://storyclue.ai appears in all meta tags
Test Charlotte's Web Chapter 1 — verify puzzle generates and validates
Test Genesis Chapter 1 — verify religious content works
Test on iPhone — verify mobile layout and keyboard behavior
Test on iPad — verify split screen fills screen correctly
Test on desktop — verify full experience
Run Google PageSpeed Insights on storyclue.ai — target score above 90
Submit sitemap to Google Search Console at search.google.com/search-console
Share live storyclue.ai URL with Bob for final review
$9.99/month — homeschool families, unlimited puzzles at storyclue.ai
Enterprise — curriculum publishers and school districts
Validation first: Post storyclue.ai live URL in one homeschool Facebook group. Ask: Would you use this? The answer tells you everything before spending another dollar.

Bob Buckmaster grew up doing Sudoku, never crosswords. Crosswords felt like something old people did. He became a numbers person. His partner Carole, an 84-year-old research librarian, does the NYT crossword daily and has a vast vocabulary built over a lifetime of reading.
If there had been a tool that made crosswords fun and relevant when Bob was young — connected to books he was already reading, written at his level — he might have become a word person too.

Carole tested the Reacher puzzle and treated it as a cognitive recall exercise without being asked. She did not want a brain training app. She wanted a puzzle about her book. The cognitive benefit was invisible — which is exactly right.
Two markets. One tool. Kids building vocabulary they do not yet have. Seniors keeping vocabulary they have spent a lifetime earning.
Domain secured: StoryClue.ai. Built in Duck Key, Florida. May 2026.
StoryClue.ai · AI Generated Crossword Puzzle Maker · Claude Code Project Brief · May 2026
